message:
feat(responsiveness): split-conformal calibration + Page-Hinkley drift detection + MAD outlier rejection for governor thresholds

Adds a "conformal" calibration mode for the indexer responsiveness
governor alongside the existing "static" mode. Instead of hand-tuned
constants for the pressured/severe load and PSI thresholds, the
governor can now derive them from a rolling window of healthy-period
samples with a finite-sample coverage guarantee per host.

Rationale: static thresholds are tuned for one reference machine and
either flap (too tight) or miss real pressure (too loose) on others.
Conformal quantiles adapt to each deployment while preserving
determinism and the contract that severe > pressured.

- CalibrationMode enum (Static | Conformal), serde-tagged so operators
  can pin the mode from config. Static remains the default — all 17
  existing governor unit tests stay on that path.
- Split-conformal quantile over a rolling window of size K (default
  256 — ~2.3 h of history at the 2 s tick). Below K_MIN (32) the
  governor falls back to static thresholds for that tick; this is
  deterministic conservative behavior, never an inverted pair.
- Two coverage levels: α=0.05 for the pressured quantile (≈ 1 FP
  step-down per 20 healthy ticks), α=0.01 for severe (≈ 1 FP
  floor-drop per 100 ticks). The tighter severe level reflects that
  dropping to the floor is more disruptive than a mild shrink.
- Page-Hinkley change-point detector (δ=0.01, λ=0.5) on the healthy
  sample stream. When cumulative drift exceeds λ the calibration
  window resets so the quantiles track the new regime instead of
  lagging behind a workload shift.
- Huber/MAD outlier filter (k=3.5 ≈ 3σ under Normal tails): samples
  are still used for the current-tick decision but excluded from the
  calibration window, so a single burst does not poison the quantile
  estimate for hours.
- Degenerate-window guard: when every calibration sample is identical
  (all-healthy or all-pinned) the two quantiles collide. The
  apply-calibration path refuses to emit in that case and silently
  falls back to static — verified by
  conformal_mode_falls_back_to_static_on_degenerate_window.
- Telemetry: calibration block on every step, reporting mode,
  load/psi window lengths, emitted q̂ values, K, drift_reset_count,
  and outliers_rejected. Serialized JSON shape pinned by
  conformal_telemetry_serializes_with_calibration_block.
- Monotonicity invariant (PO-C2-3): severe q̂ > pressured q̂ whenever
  both are emitted. Enforced by
  conformal_mode_never_inverts_severe_vs_pressured_invariant using a
  stationary sawtooth so the top-of-window samples are genuinely
  distinct.

Spec: tests/artifacts/perf/2026-04-21-profile-run/ALIEN-ARTIFACT-CARD2-SPEC.md
